channel.title: Business - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Silicon Valley is building a shadow power grid for data centers across the U.S. - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. Silicon Valley is building a shadow power grid for data centers across the U.S.  The Washington Post
  2. This AI Electricity Stopgap Needs a Big Caveat  Bloomberg.com
  3. Chinese green energy tycoon warns AI boom will strain global power  Financial Times
  4. This Daring Developer Wants To Power America’s AI Future  Forbes
  5. E&E News: US grid reliability watchdog to recommend data center rules  POLITICO Pro

item.title: ‘Fighting the AI Ghost Trade’ – Dan Ives Lists 10 Catalysts to Reverse the Tech Selloff - TipRanks
item.description:
1. ‘Fighting the AI Ghost Trade’ – Dan Ives Lists 10 Catalysts to Reverse the Tech Selloff  TipRanks
  2. Is the share market headed toward a ‘SaaS-pocalypse’ – and what would that mean?  The Guardian
  3. AI “doomsday” fears overblown, Ives says  Proactive financial news
  4. Is software dead? Reports are greatly exaggerated  Axios
  5. JPMorgan Flags 'AI-Resilient' Software Names After Selloff  Yahoo Finance

item.title: Stock Investors Brace for Possible U.S. Strike in Iran, Send Dow Industrials Falling - The Wall Street Journal
item.description:
1. Stock Investors Brace for Possible U.S. Strike in Iran, Send Dow Industrials Falling  The Wall Street Journal
  2. Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures falter as US GDP cools, Fed-favored PCE inflation heats up  Yahoo Finance UK
  3. CNBC Daily Open: U.S. markets rocked by private credit worries and potential strike on Iran  CNBC
  4. Stocks Retreat as Brent Pushes Further Above $70: Markets Wrap  Bloomberg.com
  5. Stock Market News, Feb. 19, 2026: Dow Slips; Oil Prices Hit Highest Since Summer  The Wall Street Journal
